Let $f(x)$ be the original function.
If the graph of $y = f(x)$ is shifted $30$ units to the right, the resulting graph is identical to the original graph. This means that $f(x - 30) = f(x)$ for all $x$.
We want to find the smallest positive $b$ such that if the graph of $y = f\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)$ is shifted $b$ units to the right, then the resulting graph is identical to the original graph of $y = f\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)$.
This means that $f\left(\frac{x}{3} - b\right) = f\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)$ for all $x$.
Let $u = \frac{x}{3}$. Then $x = 3u$.
So we have $f(u - b) = f(u)$ for all $u$.
Since $f(x - 30) = f(x)$, we can say that $f(x)$ is periodic with period $30$.
We want to find $b$ such that $f(u - b) = f(u)$ for all $u$.
Since $f(x)$ has period $30$, we have $f(u - b) = f(u + 30k - b)$ for any integer $k$.
We want $f(u - b) = f(u)$ for all $u$.
If $u - b = u$, then $b = 0$, which is not positive.
If $u - b = u + 30$, then $-b = 30$, so $b = -30$, which is not positive.
We want $f(u - b) = f(u)$ for all $u$.
Since $f(x - 30) = f(x)$, we can say that $f(x)$ is periodic with period $30$.
We have $f\left(\frac{x}{3} - b\right) = f\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)$.
Let $x = 3u$. Then $f\left(\frac{3u}{3} - b\right) = f\left(\frac{3u}{3}\right)$, so $f(u - b) = f(u)$.
Since $f(x - 30) = f(x)$, we have $f(u - b) = f(u)$ for all $u$.
We want $f(u - b) = f(u)$ for all $u$.
We know that $f(x - 30) = f(x)$.
Let $x - 30 = u$. Then $x = u + 30$.
So $f(u) = f(u + 30)$.
We want $f\left(\frac{x}{3} - b\right) = f\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)$.
Let $x = 3u$. Then $f\left(\frac{3u}{3} - b\right) = f\left(\frac{3u}{3}\right)$, so $f(u - b) = f(u)$.
We know that $f(u - 30) = f(u)$.
We want $f(u - b) = f(u)$.
If $u - b = u - 30$, then $b = 30$.
If $u - b = u + 30$, then $-b = 30$, so $b = -30$, which is not positive.
We want $f(u - b) = f(u)$.
Since $f(x - 30) = f(x)$, we have $f(u - b) = f(u)$ if $u - b = u - 30$, so $b = 30$.
We want $f\left(\frac{x}{3} - b\right) = f\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)$.
Let $x = 3u$. Then $f\left(\frac{3u}{3} - b\right) = f\left(\frac{3u}{3}\right)$, so $f(u - b) = f(u)$.
We know that $f(x - 30) = f(x)$.
We want $u - b = x - 30$, so $u = x - 30 + b$.
Then $f(u) = f(x - 30 + b)$.
We want $f(u) = f(u)$, which is always true.
We want $f(u - b) = f(u)$.
Since $f(x - 30) = f(x)$, we have $f(u - b) = f(u)$ if $u - b = u - 30$, so $b = 30$.

```python
print(30)
```
